[2023-01-16 15:51] elyot: I pasted an update on Steam, so I will paste the same thing here
[2023-01-16 15:51] elyot: The downtime was caused by 2 things:
(1) The hardware itself degraded and the node running the server is no longer reachable on Amazon: https://i.imgur.com/BumR4wE.png

(2) We can no longer start up new nodes of this type because Amazon Classic platform is being retired, so we need to migrate everything (including the database) over to the virtual private cloud before hosting another server.

I spent a few weeks trying to deal with (2) last summer, getting a lot of help from various folks in the Prismata community. After hitting numerous errors, we were unable to make progress. We backed up the database, but we're unable to initialize a new database from the snapshot. We purchased an expensive AWS support package and they still were unable to help us.

Some Prismata players and friends (with far more devops experience than me) have been generously offering their time to help migrate the database and get things back online. I am eternally grateful for their help, but it's quite a big effort as we have to create new servers, create a new redis cache, initialize a new db, migrate the old one, update all the deploy scripts, update the server code itself, and complete the deployment. There are many roadblocks to overcome in doing this, since a lot of the code is quite old and hasn't been maintained since 2018. The devops pros who set things up for us initially have not worked on the game in about 8 years.

I hope to have good news to share soon.

messages.content: I pasted an update on Steam, so I will paste the same thing here

If they have been unreceptive to this sentiment after being made aware in the past, then I think some community/moderator action is warranted

But if no attempt to communicate this to them was made yet, then I think that should happen first

messages.author: vanilvanil
messages.content: There's no way to police individual relations, it's easier to lay a baseline for what sort of communication is acceptable between two players. Then players who are on better terms can police their own interactions

messages.author: vanilvanil
messages.content: I wouldn't want a stranger telling me to kms either, it's just not really somethin that I can take in good faith and comfort

messages.author: vanilvanil
messages.content: Who's job is it to talk to prismata players tho. Since i assume it's coc is different to the server

messages.author: masn6811
messages.content: Personally, I think community/moderator action is already warranted. For milder forms of language, where someone might not know if it's acceptable or not, I understand that they should be communicated to first, but I think every reasonable person should know that kys is not acceptable. As such, what I want is a temporary chat-mute, because I honestly think flopflop should have known better. But again, this is just my opinion, and the mod team has the final call.

messages.author: vanilvanil
messages.content: If you wanted a mute, probably this server wasn't the right place to go?

messages.author: spiritfryer
messages.content:
If asking them to stop because it makes almost everyone uncomfortable and spreads negativity actually works, then wouldn't that be better than forced moderation?

I will admit that it's possible that it is highly likely that a person who is comfortable to use "kys" frequently, would be unresponsive to the above sentiments, but I think it's better if forced moderation only happens once a warning is shown to be ineffective, no?

messages.author: vanilvanil
messages.content: I don't think we need to ask them. I think i moderator should just tell them to stop

messages.author: spiritfryer
messages.content: And a warning being shown to be ineffective does not necessarily require a repeat offense -- it could also be justified via an unconvincing response to the warning

messages.author: masn6811
messages.content: My argument is that he should of course be asked to stop because it makes people uncomfortable, but he should also be moderated because every reasonable person should know that kys is unacceptable without having to be told that.

messages.author: spiritfryer
messages.content: Hmm, I suppose I would agree that if someone was to be receptive to stop that behavior, then they should be willing to "serve penance" as part of their resolve to change

messages.author: spiritfryer
messages.content: But at the same time I wouldn't mind if they didn't serve penance, if at the end they changed

messages.author: vanilvanil
messages.content: If someone learns their lesson the problem is over

messages.author: masn6811
messages.content: like if he said "Sorry, I didn't know kys was unacceptable language to use, I won't use it again." would you believe that?

messages.author: masn6811
messages.content: if you do believe that no moderation is needed, just being told is enough

messages.author: masn6811
messages.content: but i don't believe that at all

messages.author: spiritfryer
messages.content: Yea I get where the problem resides, but not sure what the best way to handle it, without more data or experience with similar scenarios

messages.author: vanilvanil
messages.content: The real question is

messages.author: vanilvanil

messages.content: We all know it's rude

messages.content:
Like without knowing the individual, it's hard to say what works

There could be a person who will always spread extreme negativity through chat, regardless of how many times they are muted -- then the only solution is to permanently mute them

messages.author: masn6811
messages.content: i mean i figure it's the @Mod Team 's jobs. They know the rules, and if the rules don't cover it, they can make the call to ask elyot so he doesn't get pung unnecessarily.

messages.author: spiritfryer

messages.author: vanilvanil
messages.content: Either they should've known it was wrong, or we tell them it's wrong

messages.author: spiritfryer
messages.content: Without even looking I'm sure the community guidelines already cover the idea of "don't be a shit"

messages.author: vanilvanil
messages.content: Yeah, but they are all either banned or don't come up

messages.author: spiritfryer
messages.content: So isn't the question more of what is the right way of handling a situation where someone was acting shitty?

messages.content: Yeah but i don't think it's that deep

messages.author: vanilvanil

messages.author: spiritfryer
messages.content: Maybe I am approaching it from a naive/futile perspective, of trying to work towards an environment where ppl are not shitty not due to fear of forced moderation, but just because being shitty not good

messages.author: spiritfryer
messages.content: And I guess immediate forced moderation may interfere with the potential of accomplishing the latter?

messages.author: spiritfryer

messages.author: spiritfryer
messages.content: I guess what I am trying to decide is whether it's worth pursuing a chance, even if it is small, of having the individual truly change for the better, rather than just stop due to forced moderation
